zh.lifeSummary: 當Gwilym ap Evan於1435年出生在威爾士安格萊時，他的父親Gruffudd ap Dafydd23歲，母親Mrs. Gruffudd ap Dafydd23歲。他於大約1477年與Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn在英國威爾斯安格萊波多爾根結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gwilym ap Evan于1435年出生在威尔士安格萊时，他的父亲Gruffudd ap Dafydd23岁，母亲Mrs. Gruffudd ap Dafydd23岁。他于大约1477年与Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn在英国威尔斯安格萊波多爾根结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Abt  .

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from Apt, the Yiddish name of Opatów in Świętokrzyskie Voivodeship, Poland. The placename (in German, Yiddish, and Polish) is from a root meaning ‘abbot’, the place having been named for the local abbey. Compare   Abt  .

variants.name: Abt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Horst, Johann, Kurt.

German and Dutch: from Middle Low German or Middle Dutch abt ‘abbot’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a servant of an abbot or a nickname for someone thought to resemble an abbot in some way. In some instances it may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with this word.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Abt  , a habitational name from Opatów in Poland.

variants.name: Apter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avrohom, Bronya, Eliezer, Naftali.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Apt (see   Apt  ).
English: habitational name from Aptor in Marldon, Devon, composed of Old English uppe ‘up, above’ + torr ‘rocky outcrop’.

nameMeanings.name: Evan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh (southern): from the personal name Evan, itself from Ieuan, a Welsh derivative of Latin Johannes (English John). See Evans .

variants.name: Bevan
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Iefan ‘son of Evan’, with fused patronymic marker (a)p. Compare Evan and Evans .

variants.name: Heaven
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Evan  .
English: variant of   Haven  .
Irish (Tipperary): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEimhín ‘descendant of Eimhín’, a personal name probably based on a diminutive of éimh ‘swift’, ‘prompt, ready’.

variants.name: Haven
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) harbor’, from Middle English haven (Old English hæfen ‘haven’).
Welsh (Monmouthshire): variant of   Evan  .
Dutch: variant of Haveman , or possibly a topographic name from haven ‘port, harbour’. See also De Haven .

variants.name: Evans
meanings: Welsh: derivative of Evan , from Efan, Ifan, medieval forms of Ieuan (from Latin Johannes, the source of English John), dating from c. 1500, with the post-medieval patronymic suffix -s. Welsh Sion was a separate borrowing of English John, and Evan (Ieuan) and Sion developed as independent names.

nameMeanings.name: Gwilym
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Gwilim
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Gwillym
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: Charles I of Spain became Emperor Charles V of the Holy Roman Empire.

en.lifeSummary: When Gwilym ap Evan was born in 1435, in Anglesey, Wales, his father, Gruffudd ap Dafydd, was 23 and his mother, Mrs. Gruffudd ap Dafydd, was 23. He married Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn about 1477, in Bodorgan, Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gwilym ap Evan est né en 1435, à Anglesey, Galles, son père, Gruffudd ap Dafydd, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Gruffudd ap Dafydd, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn environ 1477, à Bodorgan, Anglesey, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gwilym ap Evan 1435 in Anglesey, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gruffudd ap Dafydd 23 und seine Mutter Mrs. Gruffudd ap Dafydd 23. Er hat um 1477 in Bodorgan, Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gwilym ap Evan è nato il nel 1435 nel Anglesey, Galles, il suo padre, Gruffudd ap Dafydd, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Gruffudd ap Dafydd, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn circa 1477, nel Bodorgan, Anglesey, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ko.lifeSummary: Gwilym ap Evan(이)가 1435년 웨일스 앤글레시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gruffudd ap Dafydd(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Gruffudd ap Dafydd(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1477년 영국 웨일스 앤글레시 보도르건에서 Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gwilym ap Evan родился в 1435 г. в местности англеси, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Gruffudd ap Dafydd было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Gruffudd ap Dafydd, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn около 1477 г. в местности бодорган, англеси, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.
